programme.desc: Duane lands a huge contract for the Electra and puts enormous pressure on mechanic Adam to prep the plane without the help of his mentor Chuck.

programme.sub-title: S01 : E06 Pierogies, Bagels, and Ramen in NYC

programme.title: Worth the Hype
programme.sub-title: S02 : E03 Cubanos, Empanadas, & Smoked Beef Rib in Miami

programme.desc: From dumplings to pretzels to sushi, Stephanie finds people turning their passion for food into side hustles.

programme.title: Mad Good Food
programme.desc: Spring is in the air and Chef Derrell makes lamb shanks with lemon couscous, a savory lamb curry and gyros.

programme.title: Mad Good Food
programme.desc: Sunday Supper is served as Chef Derrell makes his signature meatballs and adds them into soup and a tasty sub.

programme.title: Mad Good Food
programme.desc: Chef Derrell shares his best Guys’ Night Out stories and favorite meal of lemon pepper wings and loaded fries.

programme.title: Mad Good Food
programme.desc: Taco Tuesday is fun Chef Derrell’s way with pernil tacos, mexican street corn, empanadas, and flambe maduros.

programme.title: Mad Good Food
programme.desc: Chef Derrell shares his favorite Mother’s Day meal of shrimp and grits, ceviche and a fancy biscuit sandwich.

programme.title: Mad Good Food
programme.desc: Chef Derrell serves up comfort and caters to the broken-hearted with lobster mac n’ cheese and tomato bisque.

programme.title: Mad Good Food
programme.desc: Chef Derrell is serving a Low-Country Boil with all the fixings and berry skillet cobbler to kick-off Summer.

programme.title: Mad Good Food
programme.desc: Chef Derrell puts the “soul” in Soul Food as he makes BBQ ribs, collard greens and potato salad.

programme.title: Mad Good Food
programme.desc: Chef Derrell makes his Thanksgiving meal of herb cornish hens, stuffing and tostones with cranberry sauce.

programme.title: Mad Good Food
programme.desc: Take-out dishes at home are on the menu as Chef Derrell makes General Tso’s chicken, bok choy and egg rolls.

programme.title: Mad Good Food
programme.desc: Steakhouse classics are served up as Chef Derrell makes steaks and delicious sides that will impress everyone.

programme.title: Mad Good Food
programme.desc: Chef Derrell makes a holiday dinner fit for Royals or fancy family guests with crown roast and potato gratin.

programme.title: Mad Good Food
programme.desc: A traditional fish fry is served and Chef Derrell Smith shares dishes and cultural takeaways for all to enjoy.

programme.title: Mad Good Food
programme.desc: Burgers, shakes and tater tots are bringing back childhood memories and diner favorites for Chef Derrell.

programme.title: Let Frankie Cook
programme.sub-title: S01 : E01 The Wonderful World of Pizza
programme.desc: Frankie makes pizza magic with two doughs, from Grilled Pizza to Grandma slices to honey-drizzled dessert.

programme.title: Let Frankie Cook
programme.desc: Frankie goes full on Italian steakhouse with Treviso salad, ice cold martinis, and Bistecca alla Fiorentina.

programme.title: Let Frankie Cook
programme.sub-title: S01 : E03 Lunch That Goes the Distance
programme.desc: Frankie bikes with Date Balls, forages ramps for pasta, and serves Juicy Lucy burgers with his friend Will.

programme.title: Let Frankie Cook
programme.desc: Frankie explores his pasta roots with Cavatelli, ragout Pappardelle, anchovy Gnocchi, and squash Orzotto.

programme.title: Let Frankie Cook
programme.sub-title: S01 : E05 Getting the Band Back Together
programme.desc: Frankie jams with his band and makes Shoyu Ramen, Tare Eggs, Chicken Meatballs, and a crispy Okonomiyaki.

programme.title: Let Frankie Cook
programme.sub-title: S01 : E06 Pancake Stacks and Snacks for Two

programme.title: Struggle Meals
programme.desc: Frankie’s favorite cookout classics take you from weekend picnic to backyard BBQ, with or without a grill.

programme.title: Struggle Meals
programme.sub-title: S05 : E02 Bring Your Own Lunch
programme.desc: These handheld lunches for work or school are easy on the wallet and customizable for the whole family.

programme.title: Struggle Meals
programme.desc: With a little mixing & matching, Frankie shows us how versatile, fun and fulfilling a homemade salad can be.

programme.title: Struggle Meals
programme.sub-title: S05 : E04 How Do You Like Them Apples
programme.desc: From sweet treats to savory meals, explore all the possibilities apples have to offer.

programme.title: Struggle Meals
programme.desc: Don’t overlook the humble squash’s superpowers in these delicious dishes that’ll feed you year around.

programme.title: Struggle Meals
programme.sub-title: S05 : E06 Spooktacular Tricks and Treats
programme.desc: Frankie’s got tricks and treats up his sleeve this Halloween with simple, wicked good dishes for all to enjoy.

programme.title: Struggle Meals

programme.title: Struggle Meals

programme.title: Room to Improve

programme.title: Room to Improve

programme.desc: Dermot is in Templetuohy, Co. Tipperary with Joanne and Sean Cleere who believe they need a substantial extension but Dermot doesn't actually concur.

programme.desc: Dermot is in Laytown, co.Meath, with a budget of 60,000 euros, Dermot's worry "is even if you took away the shoddy workmanship you'd spend the 60k, this is a really difficult one..."

programme.title: Journey Within S1 - "Malaysia"
programme.desc: Marilia explores social support at Kechara Soup Kitchen, tries Acroyoga, enjoys plant-based food at Nomad Urban Cafe, and visits Melawati Canine Sanctuary. Her experiences emphasize the importance of helping others and maintaining life balance.

programme.title: Journey Within S1 - "Garden Route"
programme.desc: Marilia’s journey on South Africa's Garden Route explores plant medicine, beauty in nature, and animal communication, emphasizing the profound connection between humans and the natural world.

programme.title: My Van Life S1 - "North Carolina"
programme.desc: Meet Lisa, a former lawyer who escaped the rat race and moved into a campervan after the early death of her mother. Lisa embarks on a cross-country road trip, peeking inside homes on wheels, and connecting with the incredible community of nomads.

programme.title: My Van Life S1 - "Mississippi, Alabama & Arkansas"
programme.desc: A hurricane in the south forces Lisa to change direction. We meet southern Van Lifers living under the radar in a state where the lifestyle is judged harshly. Tragically, Lisa’s story takes a heavy turn when she discovers that her father has died.

programme.title: My Van Life S1 - "Texas"
programme.desc: A colourful graffiti artist in Austin shares her story of loss with Lisa and gives her a tarot reading. We travel to rural Texas to overnight with Jupiter: an outspoken, non-binary person of colour, living in their vintage RV while studying law.

programme.title: My Van Life S1 - "California Desert"
programme.desc: Lisa connects with a nomad poet, who literally helps her ‘climb’ out of her funk by scaling a challenging peak. After taking tea with a magician, Lisa meets a paramedic single mother hoping to imbue her daughter with valuable life experiences.

programme.title: My Van Life S1 - "California Coast"
programme.desc: After driving across the country, Lisa heads to Santa Monica to hang with a Hollywood producer and check out his pimped out Mercedes Benz G-Wagon. Lisa is welcomed into the school bus home of a family of 7! Because there is always room for one more.

programme.title: Traveling with Denella S2 - "St. Lucie"
programme.desc: St. Lucie, Florida, the home to the historic city of Fort Pierce, the vibrant city of Port St. Lucie, and the pristine beaches of Hutchinson Island.

programme.title: Traveling with Denella S2 - "Saint Lucia"
programme.desc: It's a place where the people are just as sweet, warm, and welcoming….. Come along with me…to Saint Lucia and Let Her Inspire You!

programme.title: Traveling with Denella S2 - "Lafayette Part 1"
programme.desc: This is a special "two-part show" on the French Creole and Cajun people, culture, music, and food.

programme.title: Traveling with Denella S2 - "Lafayette Part 2"
programme.desc: Get ready for a high energy episode, Lafayette, Louisiana featuring Zydeco music greats Chubby Carrier and the Bayou Swamp Band.

programme.title: Traveling with Denella S3 - "Ecuador"
programme.desc: Denella treks through Ecuador visiting the equator and even swinging at the edge of the world with the tour operator, Exoticca.

programme.title: Traveling with Denella S3 - "Galapagos"
programme.desc: Denella travels to The Galápagos Islands.

programme.title: Traveling with Denella S3 - "St. John"
programme.desc: Denella "Reconnects With Paradise" in the smallest of the U.S. Virgin Islands - St. John. St. John has some of the world's most beautiful beaches.

programme.title: Maximum Foodie S1 - "Philippines: Why is Filipino food unpopular?"
programme.sub-title: S01 : E01 Philippines: Why is Filipino food unpopular?
programme.desc: Liesel embarks on a journey to the country of over 7000 islands, the Philippines. From the serene to the seedy, she combs through metro Manila discovering the food; this culinary spectrum includes bouncing around trying the popular fast food joints, meeting the local experts such as blogger Cheryl Tiu, a taste of history at the world's oldest Chinatown, an unexpected dancing session at the Spanish haunts of Manila, to rubbing shoulders with the elite Chefs of the city. All in a quest to answer just why Filipino cuisine is unheard of!

programme.title: Maximum Foodie S1 - "Thailand: US Thai Food vs Authentic-Thai, which is the winner?"
programme.sub-title: S01 : E02 Thailand: US Thai Food vs Authentic-Thai, which is the winner?
programme.desc: Liesel embarks on a journey to the country of over 7000 islands, the Philippines. From the serene to the seedy, she combs through metro Manila discovering the food; this culinary spectrum includes bouncing around trying the popular fast food joints, meeting the local experts such as blogger Cheryl Tiu, a taste of history at the world's oldest Chinatown, an unexpected dancing session at the Spanish haunts of Manila, to rubbing shoulders with the elite Chefs of the city. All in a quest to answer just why Filipino cuisine is unheard of!

programme.title: Maximum Foodie S1 - "Indonesia - Balinese cuisine: what is it all about?"
programme.sub-title: S01 : E09 Indonesia - Balinese cuisine: what is it all about?
programme.desc: Balinese cuisine is considered to be extremely complex. Different than what you find in the rest of Indonesia, Bali is further influenced by the major influx of Western tourism. Have the locals kept their traditions and cooking methods strong against the wave of tourism, or have they been catered to suit the palates of foreigners? Liesel heads to the ultimate getaway paradise island, sifts through all the yoga retreats, surfer shacks, and coffee plantations all in an effort to find iconic food - as well as track down the current foodie trend of the island.

programme.title: Karena and Kasey’s Foreign Flavours S1 - "Korea"
programme.desc: Karena and Kasey land in Seoul to master Korean BBQ, study with a Kimchi Master, and cook Kiwi-Korean fusion for a panel of elite local foodies.

programme.title: Karena and Kasey’s Foreign Flavours S1 - "Italy"
programme.desc: Karena and Kasey master Roman pasta and tripe while honoring their Māori heritage and fallen soldiers at the historic Monte Cassino battlegrounds.

programme.title: Road Less Traveled S5 - "Manipur"
programme.desc: Jonathan explores India’s polo roots in Manipur, meets a women’s polo club, discovers a horse god, and uncovers local martial arts.

programme.title: My Van Life S1 - "Arizona"
programme.desc: Lisa spontaneously joins a sober dance party on a mountain top in Sedona, and meets spiritual Van Lifers who have dramatically changed their entire existence, including former NFL player Joe Hawley, and a transformative breathwork practitioner.

programme.title: My Van Life S1 - "Utah"
programme.desc: Lisa heads to Utah, the ultimate playground for Van Life adrenaline junkies: aka Dirt Bags. Base jumper Derrick shows that by confronting death, it helps us appreciate life. We meet a solo female traveler who challenges Lisa by taking her bouldering.

programme.title: My Van Life S1 - "Nevada"
programme.desc: Lisa meets off-grid desert nomads turning to this lifestyle out of necessity rather than choice. Van Life rebels rule in Slab City, while in Nevada, Bob Wells - the godfather of Van Life - helps thousands turn to nomadic life when in dire straits.

programme.title: Karena and Kasey’s Foreign Flavours S1 - "Korea"
programme.desc: Karena and Kasey land in Seoul to master Korean BBQ, study with a Kimchi Master, and cook Kiwi-Korean fusion for a panel of elite local foodies.

programme.title: Karena and Kasey’s Foreign Flavours S1 - "Italy"
programme.desc: Karena and Kasey master Roman pasta and tripe while honoring their Māori heritage and fallen soldiers at the historic Monte Cassino battlegrounds.

programme.title: Road Less Traveled S5 - "Manipur"
programme.desc: Jonathan explores India’s polo roots in Manipur, meets a women’s polo club, discovers a horse god, and uncovers local martial arts.

programme.title: A Simple Path S3 - "LA County"
programme.desc: Yann explores LA County skateboard communities, visiting Gunter's handmade leather goods workshop, Open Gallery, Justin's recycled skateboard wood shop, Tito from Local Hearts Foundation, and Eunice a vlogger, emphasizing community and creativity.

programme.title: A Simple Path S3 - "Los Angeles"
programme.desc: Yann explores Los Angeles', visiting Kingswell Skate Shop, meeting DJ Chavez and Norman Woods. He encounters Di'Orr Greenwood's artistic skateboards, Hali Strada's fashion and Chandler Burton's unique project merging drag, puppets and skating.

programme.title: Travel Quest S1 - "Mongolia"
programme.desc: There is nothing on earth that compares to Mongolia. Here the last nomadic people range their livestock over millions of acres of grasslands. Drinking fermented mare’s milk (worst tasting drink ever), riding a yak and finding the original wild horses

programme.title: Australia's Amazing Homes S1 - "Extreme Homes"
programme.desc: Australia is an enormous continent and it has profound variations in climate. Johanna and Pete find Australian designers who have responded to the radical climate by producing some very extreme architecture.

programme.title: Australia's Amazing Homes S1 - "Vintage Twist"
programme.desc: Johanna and Pete explore a collection of Australian vintage homes that have been masterfully renovated to meet the needs of modern living without destroying the past.

programme.title: Weekend Breaks with Gregg Wallace S2 - "Madeira"
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace heads to Madeira for winter sun, whale watching, forest hikes, iconic basket rides, and a deep dive into local food and wine.

programme.title: Roads Unknown India S1 - "Bikes and Boats"
programme.desc: Jess and her ride crew navigate the stunning but rugged Konkan Coast, switching between bikes and boats while juggling luggage, riders and a lost phone. Gearshifter unexpectedly joins the team as they race toward Mumbai, determined to get Pulak to the city in time to visit his family.

programme.title: Roads Unknown India S1 - "Desert Dunes"
programme.desc: Jess and the team arrive in Mumbai, soaking in the city’s vibrant culture. After saying goodbye to Pulak, the team scrambles to catch their trains to Ahmedabad and Jodhpur. Split up along the way, they reunite in Jaisalmer where they experience quintessential Rajasthan, riding across the iconic dunes of the Thar Desert.

programme.title: Food Unknown S1 - "Philippines"
programme.desc: The nation of 7,000 islands has no problems with beach, sun, or sea; but they suffer tremendously with cuisine image. Jonathan lands in Manila to discover the top chefs are well aware of the issue and, following their advice, he launches off on an expedition to find part of the solution. Driving from headhunting tribal villages, to step-back-in-time colonial towns, to restaurants in the shadow of the American military behemoth an intriguing story slowly pieces together. In the dense jungle one treasure stays buried while another comes literally out of the woodwork. But danger always lurks.. zombie moray eels, landslide prone passes, and forbidden fields behind the rice paddies.

programme.title: Capturing Winter S1 - "Guido Perrini & Geraldine Fasnacht"
programme.sub-title: S01 : E02 Guido Perrini & Geraldine Fasnacht
programme.desc: Cinematographer Guido Perrini has created many of the most revered ski and snowboard films. We follow him and multi-talented athlete Géraldine Fasnacht on a snowboarding and base jumping adventure in some of the most stunning peaks in the Swiss Alps.

programme.title: Top Million Dollar Agent S2 - "Tricky Choice (Part 1)"
programme.desc: Laura and Steven are a couple with an expanding family, and are ready to take the next steps in home ownership. They want a large plot of land for their young children to run around, and a spacious home that reflects this new phase of their life.

programme.title: Top Million Dollar Agent S2 - "Tricky Choice (Part 2)"
programme.desc: Steve Bailey is the man for the job, and with such great homes to choose from, Laura and Steve will be hard pressed to pick just one!

programme.title: Top Million Dollar Agent S2 - "We Bought a Resort (Part 1)"
programme.sub-title: S02 : E25 We Bought a Resort (Part 1)
programme.desc: Sofie heads back to Belize to help husband and wife team Rachel and Ben size up some properties. Along with their investors, the couple are looking to purchase a resort on the island of Ambergris Caye that will provide the most bang for their buck.

programme.title: Big Little Footprints S1 - "Escape To The Rockies"
programme.sub-title: S01 : E10 Escape To The Rockies
programme.desc: We travel to the UNESCO World Heritage-listed Canadian Rocky Mountains for their stunning natural beauty, historical castles, and epic adventures. This is a destination where protecting its natural resources comes before anything else.

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun S1 - "Busiest Time of the Year"
programme.sub-title: S01 : E14 Busiest Time of the Year
programme.desc: A blocked drain halts a Spanish seafood restaurant at peak time. A French complex preps for its largest booking & two new pub owners in Spain reflect on their future.

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun S1 - "Customer Complaints"
programme.desc: Can two expats cope with their customer complaints? After a blocked drain threatened their future, have the owners of a Spanish seafood restaurant turned things around?

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun S1 - "Desert Holiday"
programme.desc: A London family relocates to run a gite in rural France. Two friends compete to open a Spanish seafront bar and Expats test a holiday complex in the Spanish desert.

programme.title: A Taste of Travel S2 - "Thailand"
programme.desc: Scotty makes a new friend in Thailand and I take to the stage in a theatrical spectacular in China.

programme.title: A Taste of Travel S2 - "Balinese kitchen"
programme.desc: David gets busy in a Balinese kitchen and Alana meets a giant Chinese local

programme.title: A Taste of Travel S2 - "Shanghai"
programme.desc: Alana crashes into Shanghai and Scotty has a whale of a time in Hervey Bay.

programme.title: House Hunt S1 - "A Seller's Market"
programme.desc: Young professionals Martin & Helena face up to the complexities of the Christchurch housing market and South Aucklanders Shandel and Steven battle spiralling prices as they search for a family home.

programme.title: House Hunt S1 - "Rollercoaster Ride"
programme.desc: Blenheim schoolteachers Joanna and Chris begin a rollercoaster ride to home ownership and in South Auckland, locals Shandel and Steven take on the investors in their quest to secure a family home.

programme.title: House Hunt S1 - "The City and the Country"
programme.sub-title: S01 : E03 The City and the Country
programme.desc: City boy Ethan Tey gears up for an Auckland auction and in Blenheim will Joanna and Chris stop fighting long enough to buy their first home?

programme.title: Journeys in India S1 - "India’s Natural Beauties: Udaipur and Jaipur"
programme.sub-title: S01 : E01 India’s Natural Beauties: Udaipur and Jaipur
programme.desc: While traveling by boat, we visit the many palaces and temples on the shore. We also tour the majestic City Palace and the Garden of the Maids of Honor. In Jaipur, we tour the Pink City, seeing treasures as the Palace of Winds and the Amber Fort.

programme.title: Journeys in India S1 - "Hyderabad: The Lake City"
programme.sub-title: S01 : E02 Hyderabad: The Lake City
programme.desc: Join us as we discover the amazing history of this ancient city through its religion, royal lineage, architecture and art. In the markets, Bill shows you how to buy souvenirs like a pro.

programme.title: Journeys in India S1 - "The Wildlife of Rajastan: Ranthambore & Keoladeo National Parks"
programme.sub-title: S01 : E03 The Wildlife of Rajastan: Ranthambore & Keoladeo National Parks
programme.desc: Ranthambore National Park is part of Project Tiger, a unique wildlife conservation program set up to insure the preservation of the magnificent Tiger. In Ranthambore we encounter the tiger along with Sambar and Blue Bull, Wild Boar and Langur Monkey.

programme.title: Top Million Dollar Agent S2 - "We Bought a Resort (Part 2)"
programme.sub-title: S02 : E26 We Bought a Resort (Part 2)
programme.desc: Each with a big personality of their own, it becomes clear this couple is not going to see eye to eye on what they want. Luckily for them, Sofie is a pro at mediating conflicts when it comes to real estate.

programme.title: Top Million Dollar Agent S3 - "Net Zero"
programme.desc: Julie and James are looking for a new home to raise their two boys. James is in the tech industry and would love an energy efficient home. Julie has her own dance studio and can't wait to get back to work after giving birth to her second son.

programme.title: Top Million Dollar Agent S3 - "Wow Factor"
programme.desc: Giulia and Scott have recently come into an inheritance and want the house of their dreams.

programme.title: The Luxury Travel Show S1 - "Ulagala, Sri Lanka - Florence"
programme.sub-title: S01 : E04 Ulagala, Sri Lanka - Florence
programme.desc: From Michael Angelo’s David in Florence to elephant safaris in Sri Lanka, this is an art lovers and outdoorsy adventure.

programme.title: The Luxury Travel Show S1 - "Algarve & Parrot Cay (Turks & Caicos Islands)"
programme.sub-title: S01 : E05 Algarve & Parrot Cay (Turks & Caicos Islands)
programme.desc: From miles of soft golden sand in Portugal’s Algarve region, to the miles of sand that surrounds Parrot Cay Island in the Turks and Caicos, this is a beach lover’s episode.

programme.title: The Luxury Travel Show S1 - "Barcelona & London"
programme.desc: If you’re a budding architect or simply an admirer of beautiful buildings this episode takes in some of best in Europe from London and Barcelona.

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun S1 - "France"
programme.desc: A pool leak threatens plans at a Spanish holiday complex. A French family questions their renovation challenge and new Spanish bar owners prepare for tourist season.

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun S1 - "French Renovation"
programme.desc: How will Spain’s latest hoteliers deal with the feedback from their guests? After tiresome work, a French renovation project hits crisis point and a family moves to the Costa del Sol to open their dream bar.

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun S1 - "Review Rankings"
programme.desc: A family faces eviction hours after arriving in Spain. In France, a couple seeks creative funding for their dream and the owners of a Spanish bar embark on a mission to climb the review rankings.

programme.title: A Taste of Travel S2 - "Maitland"
programme.desc: Alana stumbles upon the secret of eternal youth and Scotty goes under lock and key in Maitland

programme.title: A Taste of Travel S2 - "Borneo"
programme.desc: Scotty gets in touch with his ancestral roots and David get's a new friend in Borneo.

programme.title: A Taste of Travel S4 - "South Africa, Fiji"
programme.desc: Scotty is in beautiful South Africa, searching for the Big 5. Carolyne explores the wonderful ancient city of Rome. Lynton heads to the hills of Fiji and is rewarded with an amazing surprise. And Susannah is in the Big Apple, New York.

programme.title: Travel in Style S1 - "CYPRUS & IBIZA"
programme.desc: Mediterranean, Mythology, and Fusion - These are just a few things that Cyprus can offer! Ibiza is undoubtedly one of the most stunning Islands in the Mediterranean.

programme.title: Travel in Style S1 - "JOHANNESBURG & BOTSWANA"
programme.desc: In Johannesburg our crew stayed in style in the luxurious Westcliff Hotel. In Botswana, we visit three remarkable Safari camps.

programme.title: Travel in Style S1 - "GOA & SINGAPORE"
programme.desc: In Goa, the team look at the incredible wildlife and beaches of this former Portuguese Colony. Staying in Asia our team discover the presidential suite in Fullerton – Singapore.

programme.title: Khanh Ong's Wild Food S1 - "Mallacoota, VIC"
programme.desc: Chef Khanh Ong Travels to Mallacoota, Victoria to explore the seafood delicacies that the area has to offer but decides to make a few pitstops along the way. He learns how to make blue cheese and picks out some wine to match with seafood dishes.

programme.title: Khanh Ong's Wild Food S1 - "Tasmania - Part 1"
programme.desc: Chef Khanh Ong takes a ride on the Spirit of Tasmania to explore Northern Tasmania and tries hunting of a different kind, gets is hands dirty harvesting a local crop and gets an insight into cooking with a protein only available in Tasmania.

programme.title: The Delinquent Gourmet S1 - "Fez, Morocco"
programme.desc: In Fez, Morocco, not much has changed since the Middle Ages but Rodney is determined to stir things up and find tantalizing new dishes he can bring home.

programme.title: The Delinquent Gourmet S1 - "Sardinia"
programme.desc: Rodney is in Sardinia, Italy, where he finds culinary inspiration around every corner. High in the Sardinian hills, his hosts prepare their specialty - roasted suckling pig. After sitting down to an outdoor family meal (where even the dog helps himself!), Rodney samples a myrtle-flavored liquor before dancing with his gentlemen hosts.

programme.title: Chasing Monsters: El Nino S1 - "Family Values"
programme.desc: A new generation of female chargers band together to compete in a unique big wave surfing ‘expression session’ on the north shore of Oahu.

programme.title: Chasing Monsters: El Nino S1 - "Skullbase"
programme.desc: The Walsh twins reveal why water safety crews and rescue teams are the unsung heroes of big wave surfing.

programme.title: Streetfood Nomad: Kolkata S1 - "The Street Scene"
programme.desc: Kolkata’s extraordinary street-food scene has been influenced by its various conquerors and rulers. The most profound influence has been from the fabulously rich and powerful Mughals. Will reimagines classic dishes while also incorporating the unique street food flavours he encounters.

programme.title: Love On The Rocks S1 - "The Honeymoon is Over"
programme.sub-title: S01 : E13 The Honeymoon is Over
programme.desc: Tayler and Jason take on their final peak in Washington State, Mt Baker. Tayler struggles when Jason explains all the things that could go wrong, but the reality is a lot could fail if they don't come together as a team.

programme.title: Around the World in 80 Tricks S1 - "Delhi & Mumbai"
programme.desc: Pete is in Delhi and Mumbai performing magic to a variety of people, including a baffling thirst quenching trick, as well as making rings and himself vanish!

programme.title: Around the World in 80 Tricks S1 - "Manchester"
programme.desc: Pete Heat checks out some of the trendiest bars in the Mumbai, meets a famous Bollywood artist and shows you how to perform your own piece of magic at home.

programme.title: Around the World in 80 Tricks S1 - "Mumbai"
programme.desc: Pete Heat in Manchester and visits Old Trafford and for his finale; he wows a group of friends with his unique and comedic magic skills.

programme.title: 7 Days S1 - "Day 4"
programme.desc: Jalou and Kevin travel to far colder places to challenge each other in an ice climbing competition.

programme.title: 7 Days S1 - "Day 5"
programme.desc: In the Chinese desert, Kevin finds out that desert racing is difficult. While in Bolivia, Jalou wants to prove to her brother that women can be better drivers.

programme.title: 7 Days S1 - "Day 6"
programme.desc: After exploring Peru, Jalou has to get over her fear when she goes white water kayaking. In Thailand, Kevin is feeling the pressure.

programme.title: Access All Areas S1 - "Sahara"
programme.desc: Francesca is in Morocco to experience life on the edge of human capability and takes on a survival challenge walking alone amongst the sand dunes of the Sahara.

programme.title: Access All Areas S1 - "New Orleans"
programme.desc: Francesca Chiorando travels to New Orleans to explore Voodoo and learn about the aftermath of Hurricane Katrina.

programme.title: Access All Areas S1 - "Massachusetts"
programme.desc: Francesca is in the USA, in an area named The Bridgewater Triangle to discover if there’s any truth to its claim as the most paranormal area of the USA.

programme.title: Epic Trails S1 - "Winter in Banff & Lake Louise"
programme.sub-title: S01 : E06 Winter in Banff & Lake Louise
programme.desc: Eric Hanson returns to Banff National Park and Lake Louise in the snowy months to explore the backcountry by snowshoe and snowboard.

programme.title: Epic Trails S1 - "The Jordan Trail"
programme.desc: Eric Hanson heads to Jordan to explore the newly developed ‘Jordan Trail’—a 450-mile hiking trail that crosses the country and passes through some of the world’s most inspiring landscapes and historical sites, including the ancient city of Petra.

programme.title: Epic Trails S2 - "Papua New Guinea"
programme.desc: Eric Hanson visits wild and remote villages in Papua New Guinea, as he challenges himself on one of the world’s most difficult trails—the Kokoda Track.

programme.title: Travel Quest S1 - "North Pole"
programme.desc: The crew takes an icebreaker to the North Pole just in time to make a satellite phone call to wish Bill's mother a happy birthday (she is so shocked she doesn't recognize him). We also track and film the biggest predators on earth…polar bears.

programme.title: Travel Quest S1 - "Rwanda…Gorillas"
programme.desc: Bill tracks through never ending mud, heavy vegetation and steep mountains, to come face-to-face with our mighty cousins. He also traces the life, and finds the remnants, of the camp of one of his idols - Diane Fossey.

programme.title: Travel Quest S1 - "Botswana"
programme.desc: Botswana is a wildlife lover’s dream with massive herds of elephants, immense pods of hippos and some of the largest prides of lions anywhere. It’s also the home of Victoria Falls…the largest falls in the world.

programme.title: Love On The Rocks S1 - "Tying the Knot at 13,000ft"
programme.sub-title: S01 : E01 Tying the Knot at 13,000ft
programme.desc: A Romantic trip to Colorado takes a windy turn when Jason pushes Tayler, despite her hesitation, to summit Mt. Democrat in 70 mph winds and -19 degree weather.

programme.title: Love On The Rocks S1 - "A Newly-Wed Recipe for Disaster"
programme.sub-title: S01 : E02 A Newly-Wed Recipe for Disaster
programme.desc: Jason loves mountain biking. Tayler prefers to read a book. Jason is up for any adventure tossed at him. Tayler likes to be a bit more prepared. Will the adventures that await in Arkansas leave the couple questioning if they have anything in common?

programme.title: Love On The Rocks S1 - "Theodora's Room"
programme.desc: Tayler and Jason travel to Eureka Springs, AR, a town with a rich history and home to one of the most haunted hotels in America, The Crescent. Jason convinces Tayler to stay one night, but will that stay lead to a night they don't want to remember?

programme.title: Running the World S1 - "Gran Canaria"
programme.desc: Brothers Max & Benj Cave are challenged to create a run through an abandoned water park on the island of Gran Canaria.

programme.title: Running the World S1 - "Jamaica"
programme.desc: Brothers Max & Benj Cave are challenged to create a unique run in an unusal setting on the Island of Jamaica.

programme.title: Running the World S1 - "Best of ep. 7 to 11"
programme.sub-title: S01 : E12 Best of ep. 7 to 11
programme.desc: In this episode the boys look back over their time spent in Portugal India Mexico Gran Canaria and Jamaica.

programme.title: Travel with a Goat S1 - "Goat"
programme.desc: Two foodies travel through Kenya with an animal. Will they decide to have the animal slaughtered after spending three days together with it?

programme.title: Travel with a Goat S1 - "Alpaca"
programme.desc: Two foodies travel through Peru with an animal. Will they decide to have the animal slaughtered after spending three days together with it?

programme.title: Travel with a Goat S1 - "Sheep"
programme.desc: Two foodies travel through Bulgaria with an animal. Will they decide to have the animal slaughtered after spending three days together with it?

programme.title: Beyond The Beaten Path S1 - "Lake Titicaca, Peru"
programme.desc: The brothers hitch-hike from the ancient Inca capitol of Cuzco, down the spine of the Andes Mountains to Lake Titicaca. They suffer acute mountain sickness, get high on coca leaves and meet some locals as they battle their way across the Andes.

programme.title: Yangliuqing New Year Painting Art
programme.desc: On May 20, 2006, Yangliuqing New Year Paintings were named as part of China’s national intangible cultural heritage. The New Year painting tradition can be traced back many centuries, to the Ming Dynasty and beyond, reaching the height of its popularity in the Qing Dynasty. There are no restrictions on the themes of the paintings, and there is no blessing they cannot convey. Yangliuqing New Year Paintings are loved all over the country for their beautiful details and vivid colors—but most of all, for the good wishes they impart to anyone who sees them.

programme.title: Yangliuqing New Year Painting Art
programme.desc: On May 20, 2006, Yangliuqing New Year Paintings were named as part of China’s national intangible cultural heritage. The New Year painting tradition can be traced back many centuries, to the Ming Dynasty and beyond, reaching the height of its popularity in the Qing Dynasty. programme.title: The Tongliang Dragon Dance
programme.desc: The Tongliang Dragon Dance is a traditional performance art rooted in Chongqing’s Tongliang District, characterized by its elaborate dragon props and dynamic choreography. Originating in the Ming Dynasty and flourishing during the Qing, this vibrant folk tradition has regained its brilliance in modern times and earned international acclaim. The dance consists of two main branches: the dragon lantern dance—featuring forms like the Grand Wriggling Dragon, the Fire Dragon, and the Straw Dragon—and the festive lantern dance, which includes acts such as “Carp Leaps Over the Dragon Gate,” “Mudfish Eating Dumplings,” and “Three Dragons.” Steeped in cultural symbolism, the Tongliang Dragon Dance...

programme.title: The Tongliang Dragon Dance
programme.desc: The Tongliang Dragon Dance is a traditional performance art rooted in Chongqing’s Tongliang District, characterized by its elaborate dragon props and dynamic choreography. Originating in the Ming Dynasty and flourishing during the Qing, this vibrant folk tradition has regained its brilliance in modern times and earned international acclaim. The dance consists of two main branches: the dragon lantern dance—featuring forms like the Grand Wriggling Dragon, the Fire Dragon, and the Straw Dragon—and the festive lantern dance, which includes acts such as “Carp Leaps Over the Dragon Gate,” “Mudfish Eating Dumplings,” and “Three Dragons.” Steeped in cultural symbolism, the Tongliang Dragon Dance...

programme.title: From Stone to Story
programme.desc: China's profound stone carving culture dates back to the Han Dynasty (202 BC – 220 AD) and flourished during the Tang Dynasty (618 – 907 AD), evolving into a distinctive art form and unique cultural practice. Among many places famous for stone carving, Hui’an County—through generations of dedicated artisans—has risen to prominence and earned the esteemed title of "World Capital of Stone Carving," awarded by the World Crafts Council.The county’s skilled stonemasons are celebrated for their ability to transform ordinary pieces of rock into beautiful works of art. This episode shines a spotlight on the intangible cultural heritage of Hui’an stone carving and reveals how it has been...

programme.title: From Stone to Story
programme.desc: China's profound stone carving culture dates back to the Han Dynasty (202 BC – 220 AD) and flourished during the Tang Dynasty (618 – 907 AD), evolving into a distinctive art form and unique cultural practice. Among many places famous for stone carving, Hui’an County—through generations of dedicated artisans—has risen to prominence and earned the esteemed title of "World Capital of Stone Carving," awarded by the World Crafts Council.The county’s skilled stonemasons are celebrated for their ability to transform ordinary pieces of rock into beautiful works of art. This episode shines a spotlight on the intangible cultural heritage of Hui’an stone carving and reveals how it has been...

programme.title: Suzhou Fans
programme.desc: Suzhou, known as the “Venice of China,” is also famous for its traditional crafts. And one of the most elegant and exquisite crafts is Suzhou fans. Celebrated for their exquisite design and fine quality, they’re popularly associated with literati. How come Suzhou gave birth to such a variety of fans? How can we appreciate their beauty in the modern world? In this episode, we are discovering more stories behind Suzhou fans.

programme.title: Foodwise：Eating Global in Beijing
programme.desc: In this episode, Tianran He and Eva May are in search of global cuisines that can be found in Beijing, the 3000-year old capital of China. From honest farm-to-table fare, to the culinary heights of French haute cuisine, and even a cooking class and wine and dine event at a traditional courtyard home in the city center, we'll discover that in Beijing, you can taste the world.

programme.title: Foodwise：Eating Global in Beijing
programme.desc: In this episode, Tianran He and Eva May are in search of global cuisines that can be found in Beijing, the 3000-year old capital of China. From honest farm-to-table fare, to the culinary heights of French haute cuisine, and even a cooking class and wine and dine event at a traditional courtyard home in the city center, we'll discover that in Beijing, you can taste the world.

programme.title: Foodwise: Quanzhou
programme.desc: In this episode, Tianran He and Eva May are in Quanzhou, the ancient starting point of the Maritime Silk Road. From its covered arcades and Roman columns to its Southeast Asian vibe, our hosts discover that Quanzhou truly is a melting pot of world cultures. Then there’s the food: From the local noodle porridge that utilizes Silk Road spices, to fried vinegar pork and a dessert made from a special algae, there’s plenty of unique dishes to try here. Over a cup of oolong, our hosts will discover how Quanzhou’s tea changed the world, and how trade with Arab civilizations influenced a local fishing village. So join us in Quanzhou, a city that UNESCO designated as an “Emporium of the World”!

programme.title: Foodwise: Xi'an
programme.desc: In this episode, Tianran He and Eva May venture to Xi'an, an ancient city with over 3000 years of history, capital to 13 Chinese dynasties. But first, our hosts are going to carb load on street food, from glutinous rice cakes and cakes made out of persimmons, to mutton soup with flatbread and the famous biangbiang noodles. They'll also taste the rich history of Xi'an by experiencing a Qin Dynasty-inspired banquet, and try millennia-old dishes such as trout grilled on a ceramic tile and calabash chicken, a Tang Dynasty delicacy.From the Terracotta Army to the Daming Palace, residence of the Tang emperors, to a dazzling nighttime boulevard that recreates the grandeur of the Tang Dynasty,...

programme.title: Foodwise: Datong
programme.desc: In this episode, Tianran He and Angela Fu are in historic Datong. Located in northern China's Shanxi Province, some 1500 years ago Datong was the capital of the Northern Wei Dynasty, which saw nomads unite northern China and Buddhism spread throughout the country. At the renowned Yungang Grottoes where giant Buddhas were carved out of cliffs, our hosts discover Datong's multicultural past and sample the emperor's favorite cake. They seek out the remains of a Jesuit church beneath the Great Wall of China, and find in the local cuisine elements that echo the past, and modern-day recipes for thousand-year old dishes.

programme.title: Foodwise: Datong

programme.title: Foodwise: Chaozhou
programme.desc: In this episode, hosts Tianran He and Angela Fu are in Chaozhou, South China. They're on a quest to discover the secrets of its success, for Chaozhou is famed for its wealthy merchant guilds and its homegrown Teochew cuisine, which enjoys global recognition.From working as part-timers in a beef hotpot restaurant, to learning about tea ceremony and shadowing a master of Teochew cuisine, our hosts discover the best way to learn about Chaozhou, is through its food.

programme.title: Wild Wonders Ep.1
programme.desc: China's vast land features extraordinary geological diversity and climate zones. Home to 5,000 years of civilization and 1.4 billion people, it sustains the world's second-largest economy while sheltering over 2,000 nature reserves. This documentary spotlights China's natural heritage for tomorrow's possibilities.

programme.desc: The Tongliang Dragon Dance is a traditional performance art rooted in Chongqing’s Tongliang District, characterized by its elaborate dragon props and dynamic choreography. Originating in the Ming Dynasty and flourishing during the Qing, this vibrant folk tradition has regained its brilliance in modern times and earned international acclaim. The dance consists of two main branches: the dragon lantern dance—featuring forms like the Grand Wriggling Dragon, the Fire Dragon, and the Straw Dragon—and the festive lantern dance, which includes acts such as “Carp Leaps Over the Dragon Gate,” “Mudfish Eating Dumplings,” and “Three Dragons.” Steeped in cultural symbolism, the Tongliang Dragon Dance...

programme.title: The Tongliang Dragon Dance
programme.desc: The Tongliang Dragon Dance is a traditional performance art rooted in Chongqing’s Tongliang District, characterized by its elaborate dragon props and dynamic choreography. Originating in the Ming Dynasty and flourishing during the Qing, this vibrant folk tradition has regained its brilliance in modern times and earned international acclaim. The dance consists of two main branches: the dragon lantern dance—featuring forms like the Grand Wriggling Dragon, the Fire Dragon, and the Straw Dragon—and the festive lantern dance, which includes acts such as “Carp Leaps Over the Dragon Gate,” “Mudfish Eating Dumplings,” and “Three Dragons.” Steeped in cultural symbolism, the Tongliang Dragon Dance...

programme.title: From Stone to Story
programme.desc: China's profound stone carving culture dates back to the Han Dynasty (202 BC – 220 AD) and flourished during the Tang Dynasty (618 – 907 AD), evolving into a distinctive art form and unique cultural practice. Among many places famous for stone carving, Hui’an County—through generations of dedicated artisans—has risen to prominence and earned the esteemed title of "World Capital of Stone Carving," awarded by the World Crafts Council.The county’s skilled stonemasons are celebrated for their ability to transform ordinary pieces of rock into beautiful works of art. This episode shines a spotlight on the intangible cultural heritage of Hui’an stone carving and reveals how it has been...

programme.title: From Stone to Story
programme.desc: China's profound stone carving culture dates back to the Han Dynasty (202 BC – 220 AD) and flourished during the Tang Dynasty (618 – 907 AD), evolving into a distinctive art form and unique cultural practice. Among many places famous for stone carving, Hui’an County—through generations of dedicated artisans—has risen to prominence and earned the esteemed title of "World Capital of Stone Carving," awarded by the World Crafts Council.The county’s skilled stonemasons are celebrated for their ability to transform ordinary pieces of rock into beautiful works of art. This episode shines a spotlight on the intangible cultural heritage of Hui’an stone carving and reveals how it has been...

programme.title: Suzhou Fans
programme.desc: Suzhou, known as the “Venice of China,” is also famous for its traditional crafts. And one of the most elegant and exquisite crafts is Suzhou fans. Celebrated for their exquisite design and fine quality, they’re popularly associated with literati. How come Suzhou gave birth to such a variety of fans? How can we appreciate their beauty in the modern world? In this episode, we are discovering more stories behind Suzhou fans.

programme.title: Foodwise: Chongqing
programme.desc: Welcome to Chongqing, China's largest city. Located in the country's southwest, it straddles two mighty rivers and is famed for its extraordinary skyline, with high-rises mushrooming out of the hills and vertigo-inducing bridges.But Tianran He and Angela Fu are here for the food: From xiaomian noodles and Jianghu cuisine to the city's most famous dish: Chongqing hotpot, our hosts take on the spicy, numbing and scintillating aromas of Chongqing cuisine. We promise you: Chongqing will electrify your senses and tantalise your taste buds.

programme.title: Foodwise: Chongqing
programme.desc: Welcome to Chongqing, China's largest city. We promise you: Chongqing will electrify your senses and tantalise your taste buds.

programme.title: Foodwise: Veggie Beijing
programme.desc: Beijing is a 3000-year old metropolis home to a dizzying array of culinary traditions, not least of which is vegetarian cuisine. For Beijingers, eating vegetarian is the best way to welcome spring. In this episode, Tianran and Angela take up the challenge: From simple home cooking utilizing spring flowers and leaves, to the extravagant mock-meat masterpieces of imperial cuisine, and international dishes well-known to the vegetarian community, our hosts discover a plethora of plant-based options to be found amongst the historic alleys and skyscrapers of China's capital.

programme.title: Foodwise: Veggie Beijing
programme.desc: Beijing is a 3000-year old metropolis home to a dizzying array of culinary traditions, not least of which is vegetarian cuisine. For Beijingers, eating vegetarian is the best way to welcome spring. programme.title: Foodwise: Great Walls of Beijing
programme.desc: The Great Wall of China is a popular tourist destination, but did you know that in Beijing, it's where locals go to unwind? From feasts of tofu made using well-water and fish caught fresh from the reservoir, to bubbling cauldrons of thinly-sliced lamb, the hilly suburbs of Beijing are home to some of the city's best-loved weekend escapes - and they're all by the foot of the Great Wall.

programme.title: Foodwise: Great Walls of Beijing
programme.desc: The Great Wall of China is a popular tourist destination, but did you know that in Beijing, it's where locals go to unwind? From feasts of tofu made using well-water and fish caught fresh from the reservoir, to bubbling cauldrons of thinly-sliced lamb, the hilly suburbs of Beijing are home to some of the city's best-loved weekend escapes - and they're all by the foot of the Great Wall.

programme.title: Foodwise: How to Order Chinese Food
programme.desc: How do you order Chinese food? What's the best way to pair dishes? And what's up with the naming system? In this episode, hosts Tianran and Eva will give you a crash course on everything from deciphering the menu and choosing the right restaurant, to pairing dishes according to color, aroma, taste, and form, so that by the end of the show, you too can feast your friends like a pro.

programme.title: Foodwise: How to Order Chinese Food
programme.desc: How do you order Chinese food? What's the best way to pair dishes? And what's up with the naming system? In this episode, hosts Tianran and Eva will give you a crash course on everything from deciphering the menu and choosing the right restaurant, to pairing dishes according to color, aroma, taste, and form, so that by the end of the show, you too can feast your friends like a pro.

programme.title: Foodwise: Eat Like Confucius
programme.desc: "Foodwise" host Tianran He explores the ancient town of Qufu, the birthplace of Confucius. Together with a 75th generation descendant of the sage himself, they explore one of Confucius' lifelong pursuits: To cultivate oneself for the betterment of others. From the sophisticated cooking techniques and dining etiquette of Confucian cuisine, which was used for banqueting emperors and aristocrats, to getting hands on with street food and local delicacies, Tianran explores how Confucius' ideals have permeated through modern day life in Qufu.

programme.title: Foodwise: Eat Like Confucius

programme.title: Wild Wonders Ep.2
programme.desc: China's vast land features extraordinary geological diversity and climate zones. Home to 5,000 years of civilization and 1.4 billion people, it sustains the world's second-largest economy while sheltering over 2,000 nature reserves. This documentary spotlights China's natural heritage for tomorrow's possibilities.

programme.title: Ding Porcelain Firing
programme.desc: In ancient times, skilled craftsmen combined clay with fire and, using nothing more than their hands and wisdom, created the history of porcelain.A thousand years ago, at the time of the Northern Song Dynasty, a type of porcelain was produced that stood out for its exquisite quality, elegant coloring and beautiful patterns. This was Ding porcelain, and it became a favorite at the imperial court. However, within 200 years, amid the chaos of constant warfare, the art of making it was lost. The dazzling white light of the Ding kilns had faded, seemingly forever. And yet, 800 years later, that light has been reignited.

programme.title: Ding Porcelain Firing

programme.title: A Touch of Speed: The Art of Wing Chun
programme.desc: Uncover the principles of Wing Chun, a unique Chinese martial art renowned for its efficiency and directness. This program explores its legendary origins, core concepts like the center line theory, and its practical application in close-quarters combat. Witness the subtle power and intellectual depth of this sophisticated fighting system.

programme.title: A Touch of Speed: The Art of Wing Chun
programme.desc: Uncover the principles of Wing Chun, a unique Chinese martial art renowned for its efficiency and directness. This program explores its legendary origins, core concepts like the center line theory, and its practical application in close-quarters combat. Witness the subtle power and intellectual depth of this sophisticated fighting system.

programme.title: Quyang Stone Carving
programme.desc: Quyang is known as a hometown of Chinese carving. Quyang's stone carving works are popular around different parts of China, thus also exported to over 100 countries and regions worldwide. The style of Quyang stone carving both carries the mysterious and rough spirit of the Wei Dynasty and the natural and graceful forms of the Tang and Song dynasties. It reflects the life experiences and live philosophies of local artisans. Each statue's showcases unique oriental aesthetics.

programme.title: Quyang Stone Carving
programme.desc: Quyang is known as a hometown of Chinese carving. programme.title: Grand Song of the Dong — Natural Oriental Harmony
programme.desc: The Grand Song of the Dong is a polyphonic folk singing tradition of the Dong ethnic group. Performers from several villages gather together to sing, unaccompanied.The songs, which describe the history, beliefs, customs, and emotions of the Dong people, are an important part of their cultural identity and contribute significantly to their social cohesion.Generally sung in the Dong language, which has no written form, they are passed on orally from one generation to the next. Classified into various genres—including narrative and love songs, drinking and funeral songs—they are often accompanied by clapping, stomping, and dancing.Sung on occasions such as festivals, weddings, and...

In this episode, hosts Tianran and Eva will give you a crash course on everything from deciphering the menu and choosing the right restaurant, to pairing dishes according to color, aroma, taste, and form, so that by the end of the show, you too can feast your friends like a pro.

programme.title: Foodwise: How to Order Chinese Food
programme.desc: How do you order Chinese food? What's the best way to pair dishes? And what's up with the naming system? In this episode, hosts Tianran and Eva will give you a crash course on everything from deciphering the menu and choosing the right restaurant, to pairing dishes according to color, aroma, taste, and form, so that by the end of the show, you too can feast your friends like a pro.

programme.title: Foodwise: Eat Like Confucius
programme.desc: "Foodwise" host Tianran He explores the ancient town of Qufu, the birthplace of Confucius. Together with a 75th generation descendant of the sage himself, they explore one of Confucius' lifelong pursuits: To cultivate oneself for the betterment of others. From the sophisticated cooking techniques and dining etiquette of Confucian cuisine, which was used for banqueting emperors and aristocrats, to getting hands on with street food and local delicacies, Tianran explores how Confucius' ideals have permeated through modern day life in Qufu.

programme.title: Foodwise: Eat Like Confucius

programme.title: Wild Wonders Ep.2
programme.desc: China's vast land features extraordinary geological diversity and climate zones. Home to 5,000 years of civilization and 1.4 billion people, it sustains the world's second-largest economy while sheltering over 2,000 nature reserves. This documentary spotlights China's natural heritage for tomorrow's possibilities.

programme.title: Ding Porcelain Firing
programme.desc: In ancient times, skilled craftsmen combined clay with fire and, using nothing more than their hands and wisdom, created the history of porcelain.A thousand years ago, at the time of the Northern Song Dynasty, a type of porcelain was produced that stood out for its exquisite quality, elegant coloring and beautiful patterns. This was Ding porcelain, and it became a favorite at the imperial court. However, within 200 years, amid the chaos of constant warfare, the art of making it was lost. The dazzling white light of the Ding kilns had faded, seemingly forever. And yet, 800 years later, that light has been reignited.

programme.title: Ding Porcelain Firing

programme.title: A Touch of Speed: The Art of Wing Chun
programme.desc: Uncover the principles of Wing Chun, a unique Chinese martial art renowned for its efficiency and directness. This program explores its legendary origins, core concepts like the center line theory, and its practical application in close-quarters combat. Witness the subtle power and intellectual depth of this sophisticated fighting system.

programme.title: A Touch of Speed: The Art of Wing Chun
programme.desc: Uncover the principles of Wing Chun, a unique Chinese martial art renowned for its efficiency and directness. This program explores its legendary origins, core concepts like the center line theory, and its practical application in close-quarters combat. Witness the subtle power and intellectual depth of this sophisticated fighting system.

programme.title: Quyang Stone Carving
programme.desc: Quyang is known as a hometown of Chinese carving. Quyang's stone carving works are popular around different parts of China, thus also exported to over 100 countries and regions worldwide. The style of Quyang stone carving both carries the mysterious and rough spirit of the Wei Dynasty and the natural and graceful forms of the Tang and Song dynasties. It reflects the life experiences and live philosophies of local artisans. Each statue's showcases unique oriental aesthetics.

programme.title: Quyang Stone Carving
programme.desc: Quyang is known as a hometown of Chinese carving. Quyang's stone carving works are popular around different parts of China, thus also exported to over 100 countries and regions worldwide. The style of Quyang stone carving both carries the mysterious and rough spirit of the Wei Dynasty and the natural and graceful forms of the Tang and Song dynasties. It reflects the life experiences and live philosophies of local artisans. Each statue's showcases unique oriental aesthetics.

programme.title: Grand Song of the Dong — Natural Oriental Harmony
programme.desc: The Grand Song of the Dong is a polyphonic folk singing tradition of the Dong ethnic group. Performers from several villages gather together to sing, unaccompanied.The songs, which describe the history, beliefs, customs, and emotions of the Dong people, are an important part of their cultural identity and contribute significantly to their social cohesion.Generally sung in the Dong language, which has no written form, they are passed on orally from one generation to the next. Classified into various genres—including narrative and love songs, drinking and funeral songs—they are often accompanied by clapping, stomping, and dancing.Sung on occasions such as festivals, weddings, and...

programme.title: Foodwise: Chongqing
programme.desc: Welcome to Chongqing, China's largest city. Located in the country's southwest, it straddles two mighty rivers and is famed for its extraordinary skyline, with high-rises mushrooming out of the hills and vertigo-inducing bridges.But Tianran He and Angela Fu are here for the food: From xiaomian noodles and Jianghu cuisine to the city's most famous dish: Chongqing hotpot, our hosts take on the spicy, numbing and scintillating aromas of Chongqing cuisine. We promise you: Chongqing will electrify your senses and tantalise your taste buds.

programme.title: Foodwise: Chongqing
programme.desc: Welcome to Chongqing, China's largest city. Located in the country's southwest, it straddles two mighty rivers and is famed for its extraordinary skyline, with high-rises mushrooming out of the hills and vertigo-inducing bridges.But Tianran He and Angela Fu are here for the food: From xiaomian noodles and Jianghu cuisine to the city's most famous dish: Chongqing hotpot, our hosts take on the spicy, numbing and scintillating aromas of Chongqing cuisine. We promise you: Chongqing will electrify your senses and tantalise your taste buds.

programme.title: Foodwise: Veggie Beijing
programme.desc: Beijing is a 3000-year old metropolis home to a dizzying array of culinary traditions, not least of which is vegetarian cuisine. For Beijingers, eating vegetarian is the best way to welcome spring. In this episode, Tianran and Angela take up the challenge: From simple home cooking utilizing spring flowers and leaves, to the extravagant mock-meat masterpieces of imperial cuisine, and international dishes well-known to the vegetarian community, our hosts discover a plethora of plant-based options to be found amongst the historic alleys and skyscrapers of China's capital.

programme.title: Foodwise: Veggie Beijing
programme.desc: Beijing is a 3000-year old metropolis home to a dizzying array of culinary traditions, not least of which is vegetarian cuisine. For Beijingers, eating vegetarian is the best way to welcome spring. In this episode, Tianran and Angela take up the challenge: From simple home cooking utilizing spring flowers and leaves, to the extravagant mock-meat masterpieces of imperial cuisine, and international dishes well-known to the vegetarian community, our hosts discover a plethora of plant-based options to be found amongst the historic alleys and skyscrapers of China's capital.

programme.title: Foodwise: Great Walls of Beijing
programme.desc: The Great Wall of China is a popular tourist destination, but did you know that in Beijing, it's where locals go to unwind? From feasts of tofu made using well-water and fish caught fresh from the reservoir, to bubbling cauldrons of thinly-sliced lamb, the hilly suburbs of Beijing are home to some of the city's best-loved weekend escapes - and they're all by the foot of the Great Wall.

programme.title: Audio advices - Season 6 - Self-massage for the leg
programme.sub-title: S01 : E01 Self-massage for the leg
programme.desc: No time to relax and get a massage? These exercises let you relax by massaging yourself.

programme.title: Audio advices - Season 5 - Joint relaxation
programme.desc: The joints are the first parts of the body to react badly to stress, therefore it is essential to relax and relieve them as often as possible. Some ankle and knee rotations, combined with calm, controlled breathing will allow you to soothe your joints. With a few simple, well-targeted exercises, this session will bring you deep relaxation.

programme.title: Audio advices - Season 1 - Exercises to activate the muscles
programme.sub-title: S01 : E01 Exercises to activate the muscles
programme.desc: Need to regenerate your body? Take the time to make some exercices to decompress and find a peaceful harmony.

programme.title: Audio advices - Season 6 - Self-massage for the back and shoulder
programme.sub-title: S01 : E01 Self-massage for the back and shoulder
programme.desc: Bad posture and sagging shoulders are behaviors which can cause our body to suffer. MyZen TV offers a series of four exercises to massage your own back and shoulders. Practicing these simple and effective exercises can easily allow you to relax.

programme.title: Escape
programme.desc: Watch beautiful landscapes from all over the world !

programme.title: Earth from above - Season 2 - Of Forests and Men - Part 2
programme.sub-title: S01 : E01 Of Forests and Men - Part 2
programme.desc: The call of the Forest is about discovering places but above all it is about meeting those who protect this priceless heritage. Forests are essential to mankind. They capture CO2, promote the formation of clouds and therefore rain, protect the soil from erosion and provide a habitat for wildlife. From Madagascar, to France, to Cameroon, or even Eritrea, Yann Arthus-Bertrand sets out to discover the most beautiful forests, those who live in them, those who protect them and those who live off them.

programme.title: Julie's Frenchy Cookbook - Season 4 - The cheese menu
programme.desc: Today, Julie sets on the roads of France to talk about an essential product on French tables: cheese. More than a gourmet craze, more than an end-of-meal treat, cheese is associated with French gastronomy, and is also recognized as Intangible Cultural Heritage by UNESCO. With nearly 1,000 varieties (soft cheeses, cooked, blue-veined, pressed cheeses…), the French are the biggest consumers of cheese in the world!

programme.title: Escape
programme.desc: Watch beautiful landscapes from all over the world !

programme.title: My Zen Trendy - Season 2 - Martial Arts and meditation
programme.sub-title: S01 : E01 Martial Arts and meditation
programme.desc: Today, we will meet in Santa Monica in the hub of the last healthy trends. Val will make us discover Erewhon, the biggest organic and hipster supermarket in Los Angeles. She will then meet Troy, a guru of healthy consciousness, for an outdoor class that mixes martial arts and meditation. Then, on the legendary "Muscle Beach", the beach of all athletes, we will meet David, an action movie double, for a very aerial workout.

programme.title: My Zen Trendy - Season 2 - Hotel Paseo, Palm Springs
programme.sub-title: S01 : E01 Hotel Paseo, Palm Springs
programme.desc: Val takes us to discover Palm Springs, the trendy and designer city in the heart of the California desert. In the heart of a private collection, we will discover all the flora of this region: an outdoor plant museum, one of the largest collections of cacti, and even tequila flowers. Then we'll head to the Paséo Hotel, one of the zen and design hotels of Palm Springs, where we'll meet the chef of the restaurant who will concoct his signature dishes. We will end our day with a relaxing massage with crystals.

programme.title: My First Garden - Season 1 - Carving a Halloween pumpkin
programme.sub-title: S01 : E01 Carving a Halloween pumpkin
programme.desc: We meet again in the market garden of Romainville, for a special Halloween episode. In one of our first episodes, we learned how to sow a pumpkin seed. The pumpkins, which I had installed in my vegetable garden, have grown well now that we are in autumn. And that's good because today we're going to show you how to dig a pumpkin to make a beautiful Halloween lantern.

programme.title: Escape
programme.desc: Watch beautiful landscapes from all over the world !

programme.title: Vegetarian Delights - Season 1 - Potato cutlets with vegetable purée
programme.sub-title: S01 : E01 Potato cutlets with vegetable purée
programme.desc: In this recipe, we'll prepare delicious potato cutlets accompanied by a creamy vegetable purée. The cutlets are crispy on the outside with Japanese breadcrumbs and tender on the inside, while the celeriac puree adds a touch of sweetness and lightness. This dish is perfect for a comforting and tasty vegetarian meal.

programme.title: Vegetarian Delights - Season 1 - Creamy cauliflower soup
programme.desc: In this recipe, we'll prepare a creamy, comforting cauliflower and celeriac velouté. This mild, delicate soup is spiced up with condiments like paprika, garlic and oregano, and a touch of olive oil for an even more gourmet texture. An ideal dish for a light starter or a warming dinner.

programme.title: Earth in Sports - Season 5 - Episode 7
programme.desc: The TV show Earth in Sports will immerse you into the point of view of different athlete who are practicing extreme or peaceful sports at the best spot all around the world. You will discover the most beautiful landscapes and some of them are nearly impossible to reach.

programme.title: Earth in Sports - Season 5 - Episode 8
programme.desc: The TV show Earth in Sports will immerse you into the point of view of different athlete who are practicing extreme or peaceful sports at the best spot all around the world. You will discover the most beautiful landscapes and some of them are nearly impossible to reach.

programme.title: Escape
programme.desc: Watch beautiful landscapes from all over the world !

programme.title: My Zen People - Season 2 - Paul John Elliott
programme.desc: Without really knowing why, Paul Elliott will change his life from cartoonist to massage therapist. Aspired by success, money, success, he will create several clinics and will eventually lose the human aspects and lose himself. A new turn, a new meeting and Paul will finally resume his dreams of painting. Follow Paul in his tumults and his happiness found.

programme.title: My Zen People - Season 2 - Annabelle Higgins and Valérie Sirois
programme.sub-title: S01 : E01 Annabelle Higgins and Valérie Sirois
programme.desc: They are beautiful sisters, they are passionate about nature and they have a very strong common point: entrepreneurship. After years of evolution in consumer sectors, discover Annabelle and Valérie who knew how to listen to their passion to create a fitness and surf club (for Annabelle) and a zero waste grocery store (for Valérie).

programme.title: My Zen People - Season 2 - Véronick Desmarais
programme.desc: The everyday work is not always easy and it is sometimes difficult to get by. If there is one thing that Véronick has learned from her experience and those around her, it is because she is able to surpass that and to embark on her dreams and plans to become a massage therapist. You will believe in your abilities too.

programme.title: My Zen People - Season 2 - Violaine Courtois
programme.desc: Everything started from the dream of her parents, she becomes a veterinarian. In her career that she launched backwards, Violaine will meet people who will take her to a different path of life. The chance will be at the rendezvous but it is especially the courage to resume studies and create his company that will build his project. Follow the encounters and travels of Violaine that brought her to fasciatherapy and nonviolent communication.

programme.title: Earth from above - Season 3 - Nature’s Heroes: Gabon - Part 2
programme.sub-title: S01 : E01 Nature’s Heroes: Gabon - Part 2
programme.desc: The incredible diversity of flora and fauna, the fight against climate change, the balance between growth and sustainable development: these are all battles being fought on five continents. Yann Arthus-Bertrand takes us on this expedition. We're off to Gabon to discover the Ivindo National Park and Port-Gentil, then on to Sri Lanka in Kandi. Back to Gabon we go to Libreville, then Lambaréné to talk about turtles and the health of the local people. Finally, off to Dhaka-Bangladesh.

programme.title: Audio advices - Season 5 - After sitting for a long time
programme.sub-title: S01 : E01 After sitting for a long time
programme.desc: Is work stressing you out? Are you tired of the routine? Here's a series of relaxation exercises to help you unwind 100%.

programme.title: Audio advices - Season 5 - Lower body relaxation
programme.desc: This exercise aims to release all the tension you can feel in your legs and feet. You will first be invited to quickly relieve the upper body so you can then concentrate more easily on the rest. Then, with a series of muscle contractions and decontractions, you will deeply relax your legs and feet. You'll notice you'll feel much better overall.

programme.title: Audio advices - Season 1 - Get in contact with your needs
programme.sub-title: S01 : E01 Get in contact with your needs
programme.desc: Need to regenerate your body? Take the time to make some exercices to decompress and find a peaceful harmony.

programme.title: Escape
programme.desc: Watch beautiful landscapes from all over the world !

programme.title: Earth from above - Season 3 - Nature’s Heroes: Gabon - Part 1
programme.sub-title: S01 : E01 Nature’s Heroes: Gabon - Part 1
programme.desc: How can we reconcile the development needed to sustain the lives of nearly seven billion people with the protection of the Earth's natural resources? Yann Arthus-Bertrand and his team have chosen Gabon to continue their survey of the planet. We'll be discovering the Kongou Falls, the Langoué clearing with their elephant in Gabon. Then in Colombia, we will go on the island of Malpelo with their shark. Back to Gabon we will discover the Minkébé National Park and the bats in Franceville. We will also go to India in Hyderabad, which has convinced farmers to use no pesticides and no GMOs in their cultivation.

programme.title: Julie's Frenchy Cookbook - Season 4 - Citrus flavors
programme.desc: Julie takes you through the Pyrénées Orientales, then Corsica, before making a stop on the Côte d'Azur, and concludes her journey between Rungis and Paris... all to explore the world of citrus fruits! These fruits add flavor, zest, and seasoning to any recipe, making them indispensable in the kitchen. They bring the warmth and sunshine of the South of France and the exoticism of distant lands. While we're all familiar with lemons, clementines, and grapefruits, Julie introduces us to a thousand other varieties.

programme.title: Escape
programme.desc: Watch beautiful landscapes from all over the world !

programme.title: My Zen Trendy - Season 2 - Topanga Canyon, AMBA Yoga
programme.sub-title: S01 : E01 Topanga Canyon, AMBA Yoga
programme.desc: Val goes to the heart of Topanga Canyon, California, a favorite spot for yoga addicts and nature lovers. Here she will experience AMBA Yoga, a kind of hollistic yoga for women. We will also attend a Sound Bowl session, a Tibetan practice considered the ultimate in meditation. In the heart of Venice Beach, we will discover the "Plant Lab", a new high-tech application that records the vibrations of plants to create music. Finally, Val will present us the largest concept store of rare crystals in South America.

programme.title: My Zen Trendy - Season 2 - Hot Springs
programme.desc: Still in Palm Springs, we will discover the Parker Hotel, the most trendy and design hotel in town. A French chef will take us to cook in the middle of nature. Then, Val will go to Desert Springs, a town known for its natural hot springs. At one of the many spas in town, Val will test one of the springs and even attend a Sound Bowl session.

programme.title: My First Garden - Season 1 - Growing mushrooms at home
programme.sub-title: S01 : E01 Growing mushrooms at home
programme.desc: We meet at the Romainville market garden centre to learn how to grow mushrooms at home. Some varieties, such as button mushrooms, oyster mushrooms or shiitake mushrooms, grow quite easily in mushroom houses. But you too can do it. And today we will show you how to make a miniature version of this kit to grow oyster mushrooms at home.

programme.title: Escape
programme.desc: Watch beautiful landscapes from all over the world !

programme.title: Vegetarian Delights - Season 1 - Mashed potatoes with vegetable gravy
programme.sub-title: S01 : E01 Mashed potatoes with vegetable gravy
programme.desc: In this recipe, we prepare mashed potatoes in vegetable stock. The mashed potatoes are soft and melt-in-the-mouth, while the richly flavored broth of bell bell pepper, carrot, zucchini, celeriac and tomato enhances the whole.

programme.title: Vegetarian Delights - Season 1 - Sweet potato bruschetta
programme.desc: In this recipe, we reinvent the classic bruschetta by replacing the bread with a roasted sweet potato base. Garnished with tomatoes, guacamole, chilli pepper and a drizzle of olive oil, this healthy and tasty bruschetta is perfect for an aperitif or a light starter.

programme.title: Earth in Sports - Season 5 - Episode 11
programme.desc: The TV show Earth in Sports will immerse you into the point of view of different athlete who are practicing extreme or peaceful sports at the best spot all around the world. You will discover the most beautiful landscapes and some of them are nearly impossible to reach.

programme.desc: The TV show Earth in Sports will immerse you into the point of view of different athlete who are practicing extreme or peaceful sports at the best spot all around the world. You will discover the most beautiful landscapes and some of them are nearly impossible to reach.

programme.title: Escape
programme.desc: Watch beautiful landscapes from all over the world !

programme.title: My Zen People - Season 4 - Carine (Nice)
programme.desc: After twenty years as a lawyer, Carine decided to give in to her desire to help others heal. Her inner journey led her to discover a passion for naturopathy as she rediscovered her love for nature and learned the healing qualities of the plants around her. Combined with a deep understanding of astrology and nutrition, she is now able to help others live better and find their own path.

programme.title: My Zen People - Season 4 - Virginie (Lyon)
programme.desc: Virginie’s beautiful handwritten lettering decorates the walls and windows of many shops in and around Lyon, France, as she lives out her passion of finding beauty in every stroke. A former waitress, Virginie was struggling to find her calling when she suffered a life-altering horse accident that almost left her handicapped. At only 30, she was forced to put her whole life into perspective. She finally found herself through a love for graphic design and lettering, combined with a desire for freedom that came with working independently. To motivate other women, she has also launched “Ladyboss”, a network of inspiring women entrepreneurs.

programme.title: My Zen People - Season 4 - Laetitia (French Alps)
programme.desc: While pregnant with her daughter, Laetitia, a former journalist, experienced a strong desire to change lifestyles and move to the countryside. Remembering the teachings of her grandmother, she began to venture outdoors into the surrounding Alps and study the different plants at her fingertips. Today she holds a wealth of knowledge about the medicinal and aromatherapeutic value of plants that she applies to making natural products. With utmost respect for the environment, Laetitia gathers only what she needs in order to make herbal infusions.

programme.title: My Zen People - Season 4 - Louise (Oleron Island)
programme.desc: At only 20 years old, Louise is full of confidence and already achieved a personal dream of hers to share her passion for nutritious and locally-sourced ingredients on the island of Oleron, France. Repurposing a camping car, she set up a brunch and tea-time spot where the guests can hand-pick their ingredients in the adjacent organic fields. Louise transforms the ingredients into favourite creations of hers and even joins her father for boat trips when she needs to stock up on fresh fish for the menu!

programme.title: Earth from above - Season 3 - Japan: Mass consumption - What the labels forget to mention - Part 1
programme.sub-title: S01 : E01 Japan: Mass consumption - What the labels forget to mention - Part 1
programme.desc: In this instalment, Yann Arthus-Bertrand lifts the veil on the products people buy. Almost everything we buy has a direct impact on our environment. We head to Japan to talk about the impact of new technologies, then to the Republic of Congo in Bukavu for the coltan harvest. How does our total consumption affect the environment? What permanent brands are we imposing on our planet? How can we differentiate between useful and useless purchases?
